note: Layout Paragraphs provides a paragraphs field widget + formatters, a Paragraphs Behavior plugin that turns a paragraph type into a Layout Discovery section, a tempstore-backed builder, and an event-based extension API. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded): they probe documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site I want editors to arrange paragraphs with drag-and-drop layouts on a paragraph reference field, and render the result on the front end. Which field widget plugin id do I select on Manage form display, and which formatter plugin id renders paragraphs wrapped in their layouts?
evals.expected_output: Use the 'layout_paragraphs' field widget (multiple_values, for entity_reference_revisions fields) on Manage form display, and the 'layout_paragraphs' field formatter on Manage display. An experimental 'layout_paragraphs_builder' formatter adds front-end editing.

evals.prompt: How do I make a paragraph type act as a layout 'section' in Layout Paragraphs, and where do I choose which core layouts it may use?
evals.expected_output: Enable the paragraph type's 'layout_paragraphs' Paragraphs Behavior plugin; its settings form has an 'available_layouts' checkbox list of Layout Discovery layout plugin ids the section can use.

evals.prompt: Which Drupal modules must be installed for Layout Paragraphs to work, and where do the actual layouts (one column, two column, etc.) come from?
evals.expected_output: It depends on the paragraphs module and core's layout_discovery module; layouts come from core Layout Discovery (LayoutPluginManager), not from Layout Paragraphs itself.

evals.prompt: In a custom module I want to limit which paragraph types appear in the 'Choose a component' dialog for a particular region of a Layout Paragraphs layout. Which event do I subscribe to, and which method sets the allowed types?
evals.expected_output: Subscribe to LayoutParagraphsAllowedTypesEvent (event name 'layout_paragraphs_allowed_types'); read getTypes()/getRegion()/getParentUuid()/getLayout() and call setTypes() with the filtered array.

evals.prompt: I want to preset the paragraph type or default field values used when a new component is created in the Layout Paragraphs builder. Which event handles that?
evals.expected_output: LayoutParagraphsComponentDefaultsEvent (name 'layout_paragraphs_component_defaults'); use setParagraphTypeId() and setDefaultValues() to alter the new component.

evals.prompt: In the Layout Paragraphs field widget settings, which two options control how deeply sections can nest and whether every top-level component must be inside a layout? Give the config keys and defaults.
evals.expected_output: nesting_depth (default 0) sets maximum section nesting; require_layouts (default 0) forces all root components to live inside a layout section. Both are on field.widget.settings.layout_paragraphs.

evals.prompt: What is the route name and path of the Layout Paragraphs module settings (labels) form, and what permission does it require?
evals.expected_output: Route layout_paragraphs.label_settings at /admin/config/content/layout_paragraphs/labels, requiring the 'administer site configuration' permission; it edits the layout_paragraphs.settings config object.

evals.prompt: When editing a Layout Paragraphs layout in the drag-and-drop builder, which PHP object represents the whole layout and which service holds the in-progress (unsaved) edits?
evals.expected_output: The LayoutParagraphsLayout domain object represents the layout; the layout_paragraphs.tempstore_repository service (LayoutParagraphsLayoutTempstoreRepository, backed by the private tempstore) holds in-progress edits.
